promotions.behavior_rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat this as an installer bug, not a path or quoting bug.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Normalize existing config text before section removal and block append.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Validate the final DinoBrain TOML block before and after writing, and reject bare CR bytes at the file level.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Bump patch version to 2.2.1 and tag it because this affects installer safety.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Fixed the installer Codex config writer so malformed carriage returns cannot corrupt <machine-local-path> Added CRLF normalization before TOML block rewriting, byte-level bare CR checks after write, DinoBrain TOML block shape validation, and a regression case that starts from a malformed config containing CRCRLF and bare CR. Bumped DinoBrain to v2.2.1 and pushed main/tag.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Version bumped to 2.2.0 for closed-loop compounding semantics.
In similar future work, apply this decision: finish_task runs runCompoundingCycle when DINOBRAIN_AUTO_COMPOUND=1, before auto_sync.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Installer and current Codex config/hook now set DINOBRAIN_AUTO_COMPOUND=1 along with auto growth and auto sync.
verify:os now fails if Codex/Claude hook or MCP env misses auto-compound wiring.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Knowledge compounding proof requires a promoted behavior rule to be retrieved by a later Context Pack and memory-on behavior lift to be positive.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Implemented DinoBrain OS v2.2.0 closed-loop compounding: finish_task can run automatic trace-to-behavior-rule distillation, cleanup invalid/duplicate behavior rules, refresh retrieval indexes, and keep auto sync enabled from installer/Codex hook config. Added verify:compounding to prove later memory search/context packs retrieve the promoted rule and memory-on behavior beats memory-off baseline.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Add auto_sync as a separate policy-enforced tool instead of changing git_sync dry-run semantics.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Enable auto growth and auto sync by installer/env configuration, while blocked raw conversation and secret-bearing paths remain excluded from automatic commits.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Move hook auto_sync after codex_preflight_completed event so the preflight completion event is included in the auto-sync commit.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Check GitHub Actions release run for tag v2.1.0 if release asset availability must be confirmed from GitHub UI.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Implemented DinoBrain OS v2.1.0 closed-loop automation: hard pre-response hook block metadata, hook-side auto sync after preflight completion, finish_task auto-growth records, policy-based auto_sync commit/push tool, search_memory alias, installer env wiring for auto growth/sync, current Codex config/hook env update, and flow audit verification showing all 8 user-flow steps verified.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Classify current DinoBrain as a compounding-capable foundation, not a fully autonomous compounding OS yet.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Define true compounding as repeated conversion of conversations into reviewed preferences, decisions, claims, corrections, and behavior rules that measurably improve future sessions.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Fix live version drift so the running MCP reports v2.0.2 after reload/restart.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat installed DinoBrain as configured and probe-verified, not proven fully live for the current active Codex turn until a fresh prompt after hook approval/reload produces codex_prompt_submitted and codex_preflight_completed without manual probe.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Report the 8-step loop as partially enforced: steps 2, 3, 5, 6 are directly verified; step 1 is configured and probe-verified; steps 4, 7, 8 have known automation/policy limits.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Start a fresh Codex thread after hook approval/reload and verify that codex_prompt_submitted and codex_preflight_completed appear before any manual MCP call.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Verified the installed/current DinoBrain OS loop against the user's 8-step flow. Codex MCP registration, UserPromptSubmit hook registration, hook simulation/probe, task/context pack creation, wiki_search, finish_task support, v2 verification, and git remotes are present. The complete loop is partially rather than fully autonomous: live current-thread hook injection was not observed before manual probe, model behavior cannot be runtime-enforced by DinoBrain, search_memory is not a separate tool, knowledge growth beyond accepted instances is not fully automatic, and git_sync remains dry-run/manual-review by policy.
In similar future work, apply this decision: App repo push authorized by user and completed on main.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Pushed v2.0.2 tag because release-windows workflow is tag-triggered for v* tags.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Did not commit or push dinobrain-data because git_sync dry-run classified all 87 data changes as conditional/requires_review.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Bumped DinoBrain to v2.0.2 because retrieval and gate semantics changed materially.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Dense retrieval now reports hybrid_contextual_v2 only when a usable dense vector index/provider exists for the query; otherwise it reports lexical_fallback_v2.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Kept candidate_source labels stable while bumping underlying wiki/sqlite index versions to v3.
In similar future work, apply this decision: os_gate no longer trusts has_context_pack/context_item_count self-report without a verified context pack trace.
In similar future work, apply this decision: audit_memory_use now separates declared artifacts from event-observed artifacts.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Review whether dinobrain-data index/task changes should be committed under the existing data-vault policy.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Released as v2.0.1 because this is a stability patch on top of v2.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Data repo runtime logs and indexes were not committed or pushed automatically.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Claude Code integration now writes a UserPromptSubmit hook in addition to MCP registration; official Claude Code docs define UserPromptSubmit as firing before Claude processes a prompt.
After installing v2.0.1 on another PC, restart/trust Codex and Claude hooks so active sessions reload the new pre-response hook config.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Implemented and deployed DinoBrain v2.0.1 stability fixes based on the QA review. Patched fail-closed Codex hook duplicate-lock behavior, added Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook registration and verification, expanded v2 action gates to the full OS tool contract, made behavior eval strict when golden cases are missing, added source chunk redaction/truncation guards, made node lifecycle apply real archive/hold/merge/provenance repair operations, handled os_begin_task context-pack failures as fail-closed task/gate records, fixed Observatory JSON graph fallback for v2 indexes, bumped app/installer version to 2.0.1, added regression coverage, pushed main and tag v2.0.1. Local release ZIP was built; direct GitHub Release upload could not run because no GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN is available in this shell.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Review remained read-only; no code changes were made.
In similar future work, apply this decision: v2 stability risk is mostly in fail-closed enforcement, automatic lifecycle execution, strict eval gating, and active-session MCP/tool reload guarantees.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Patch hook duplicate-lock path to inject fail-closed unless a concrete sibling preflight report/context can be read.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Completed stability-focused QA/code review of DinoBrain v2 changes. Findings: Codex hook duplicate-lock path can skip preflight instead of fail-closing; Claude Code integration is MCP-only and does not enforce pre-response OS context; action-gate required-tool checks still cover only legacy tools; node lifecycle apply=true writes review records rather than executing merge/hold/delete/provenance repair; behavior eval exits ok when golden file is missing; Observatory JSON graph fallback rejects v2 wiki index when SQLite is unavailable; create_source_chunk stores raw chunk_text without redaction/size bounds; os_begin_task can leave started tasks if context-pack construction throws. Type check passed after adding portable Node directory to PATH; eval:behavior currently returns ok/skipped because behavior-golden.json is missing.
Use os_begin_task as the default v2 pre-response entrypoint for hooks and agents.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Fail closed when OS context is missing or destructive-risk gates block the request.
Use local deterministic hybrid retrieval: contextual chunks, BM25 sparse, dense-lite lexical similarity, RRF, provenance and root-intent reranking.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat direct user corrections as accepted behavior memory through record_feedback_correction.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Implement lifecycle actions as review records rather than silent destructive mutation.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Run installer build/release after review so other PCs install the v2 OS contract.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Run git_sync dry-run before syncing the data repo; do not auto-push data without review.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Implemented DinoBrain OS v2 foundations: package/server/installer version bumped to 2.0.0, added fail-closed os_begin_task pre-response entrypoint, switched default retrieval to hybrid_contextual_v2, added action gates, node lifecycle application, durable source chunks/provenance links, feedback correction writeback, behavior evaluation, v2 Observatory status, and v2 regression verification.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep DinoBrain as the product name, but remove graph-level dinosaur and fossil visual metaphors.
Use semantic cluster/layout naming instead of pose or silhouette terminology.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep detail access through hover and search rather than always-on long labels.
Use semantic cluster anchors for live activity, wiki, sources, projects, instances, operations, context packs, tags, and archive/error records.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep dinosaur/fossil character as low-contrast canvas scaffold rather than node placement constraint.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Default labels now favor hub labels; active task and context-pack labels are available through hover/search rather than always visible.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Used DinoBrain task/context preflight before analysis per AGENTS.md.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Relied on local git state plus GitHub PR metadata as the strongest recent evidence.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Discarded weaker candidates because Gmail search lacked scope, Calendar had no DinoBrain events, and the observatory branch was already contained in main.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Used DinoBrain context plus local git/GitHub evidence as primary signals.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Excluded Gmail-based suggestions because the connected Gmail search scope is insufficient.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Excluded calendar-based suggestions because no project-related upcoming events were found.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Kept only suggestions tied to fresh unresolved repo work, not already-landed tasks.
Use GitHub Actions with repository GITHUB_TOKEN for release asset upload so local GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN is no longer required for normal tagged releases.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep installer AppRef and DataRef on main for standard installs.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Exclude local screenshot attachment folder from git staging.
In similar future work, apply this decision: For future releases, push main, tag vX.Y.Z, and the release workflow should build/upload assets automatically.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep data/API/server-side logic unchanged; this pass is visual rendering only.
Use deterministic fossil pose locking so live graph data remains visible but no longer flattens the dinosaur silhouette.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat v13 as locally QA-passed, but do not deploy until the user approves release.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Took the DinoBrain Observatory dinosaur graph one step further after the v7 candidate. Implemented deterministic fossil mode with locked node poses, explicit slot arrays for silhouette parts, redistributed live node types across body/tail/neck/head/leg roles, reduced background edge prominence, added fixed canvas guide routes for the fossil scaffold, tuned head/neck proportions, captured v13 evidence, and updated design-qa.md to final result: passed. No deployment or commit was performed.
Do not deploy this visual automatically because the side-by-side comparison still differs from the provided reference.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep the implementation live-data-driven by using actual nodes, including root category nodes, rather than adding fake decorative node assets.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Record the design result as candidate / needs review in design-qa.md.
In similar future work, apply this decision: User should review the v7 candidate screenshot or local Observatory at http://127.0.0.1:3951/.
In similar future work, apply this decision: If the target is closer reference fidelity, implement a graph-only presentation mode with deterministic slotting for every live node before release.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Improved the DinoBrain Observatory dinosaur graph candidate after user feedback that the previous deployed visual felt disappointing. Kept changes visual-only: increased graph canvas height, added aspect-preserving dino frame mapping, redistributed root/category nodes into head/neck/tail/leg roles, strengthened pose lock, reduced edge physics, and added live-data scaffold routes. Updated design QA with v7 screenshots and marked the result as a review candidate rather than release-ready.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Released as v0.1.6 so the install ZIP and visible version are not confused with v0.1.5.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Kept .codex-remote-attachments untracked and excluded from deployment.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Used Windows Credential Manager only in-process for GitHub Release API upload and did not print the credential.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Verified remote release ZIP by downloading the uploaded asset through GitHub API and comparing SHA256.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Design-only scope: no OS, MCP, SQLite, hook, installer, or data-model behavior changed.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Promoted the graph panel to a full-width first-screen visual surface and moved detail panels below it.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Used browser-side pose targets rather than altering source graph data, preserving live node and edge semantics.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Reduced active-task label density so the silhouette stays readable.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Review the local Observatory at http://127.0.0.1:3951/ and decide whether the next iteration should add a graph-only presentation mode for a more literal full-viewport dinosaur view.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat this as design-only work: no logic, data model, hook, installer, SQLite, or MCP behavior changes.
Use the supplied image as the visual target: dinosaur silhouette made from existing graph nodes and edges, not a decorative overlay.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Wait for user confirmation before implementation.
After confirmation, inspect current Observatory rendering code and CSS, then produce a scoped design plan before editing only visual/rendering code.
Do not edit trusted_hash or local Codex hooks.json; fix the upstream installer behavior instead.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Preserve existing UserPromptSubmit groups and remove only DinoBrain hook entries before re-adding DinoBrain to the first existing group.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep PR scope to install.ps1 and scripts/verify-installer-hooks.ps1 only.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Fixed installer Codex UserPromptSubmit registration so DinoBrain is merged into the first existing prompt-hook group instead of being appended as a separate second group. Only install.ps1 and scripts/verify-installer-hooks.ps1 were changed. Added regression cases preserving why-was-fable-banned and verifying DinoBrain appears exactly once in the first UserPromptSubmit group. Created branch fix/codex-userprompt-hook-merge, committed, pushed, and opened PR #1.
Use a shared TypeScript graph-health module so Observatory, CLI refresh, and audit_memory_use rely on the same health model.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Treat an empty LLM Wiki graph as degraded rather than merely warning because the user's goal is compounding session-derived knowledge.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep data repo sync as dry-run/manual-review only per project policy; release app repo separately from operational memory changes.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Publish v0.1.5 from commit 39f3d608cdf28e285328f3fb54ab8846a8d99e06 and verify the private GitHub Release asset through the asset API rather than relying on browser download URLs.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Review DinoBrain data repo changes before any data commit/push; git_sync classified all 32 current data changes as requires_review.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Plan v0.1.5 should be Observatory-first rather than installer-first.
In similar future work, apply this decision: The upgrade should make DinoBrain visibly prove hook operation, context usage, memory lifecycle, graph integrity, and sync risk.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep user trust approval and data sync manual-review boundaries intact.
Do not bypass Codex hook trust. The helper only opens the /hooks flow and requires the user to click trust/approve.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Default installer behavior launches the hook approval helper after hook registration; users can opt out with -SkipCodexRestartFlow or the GUI checkbox.
In similar future work, apply this decision: The approval helper restarts only stale Codex desktop processes whose StartTime predates hooks.json, not arbitrary codex command runner processes.
In similar future work, apply this decision: New hook approval launcher is included in purge uninstall cleanup.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Install from v0.1.4 ZIP on the target PC; after the helper opens Codex, run or paste /hooks and approve DinoBrain.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Implemented a post-install Codex hook approval flow. The installer now creates DinoBrain Codex Hook Approval.cmd and launches it after hook registration unless skipped. The helper detects stale Codex desktop processes, restarts/reopens Codex, copies /hooks to the clipboard, and shows instructions while preserving the requirement that the user manually trusts the hook. Updated the Windows setup UI with a checked-by-default approval flow option, added verification, updated uninstall cleanup, bumped version to 0.1.4, pushed main/tag, uploaded the v0.1.4 release ZIP, and verified the uploaded private asset hash.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Git push and GitHub Release asset upload use different authentication paths in this environment.
In similar future work, apply this decision: For this PC, use git credential-manager get rather than git credential fill when the release script needs a GitHub token.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Private release asset verification should download through the GitHub asset API URL with Accept: application/octet-stream, not browser_download_url.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Rechecked GitHub release upload after the user noted GitHub updates had worked previously. Found the earlier miss: git credential fill did not expose credentials, but git credential-manager get did return the GitHub token for the logged-in clockmansy account. Used that token only in-process to run npm run release:win -- -Tag v0.1.3 -ReplaceAsset. Created the GitHub release, uploaded DinoBrainSetup.zip and DinoBrainSetup.zip.sha256, then verified by downloading the private asset through the GitHub asset API and comparing SHA256 hashes.
Do not forge or bypass Codex hook trust approval; treat it as a required user security decision.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep automating safe setup surfaces: hook registration, features.hooks=true, installed-hook handshake, duplicate prevention, and diagnostics.
In similar future work, apply this decision: A future improvement can guide the user to /hooks or add a post-install trust checklist, but not silently approve trust.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Explained that Codex hook trust approval should not be automated because it is the security boundary for command-running hooks. Verified local DinoBrain docs/code state: installer can register user-level hooks, enable hook runtime, run an installed-hook handshake, and provide diagnostics, but cannot bypass Codex's hook trust prompt.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Reinstalling over the same folder is supported only when app and data folders are DinoBrain-created Git checkouts; non-git folders are refused instead of overwritten.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Reinstall refreshes repos, rebuilds indexes, and rewrites registrations without deleting dinobrain-data.

In similar future work, apply this decision: Full purge uninstall requires an explicit DELETE DINOBRAIN confirmation unless -Yes is passed, and removes app repo, data repo, portable Node, DinoBrain launchers, and DinoBrain config/hook backups.
In similar future work, apply this decision: The Windows release path now packages DinoBrainSetup.zip plus DinoBrainSetup.zip.sha256 by default because direct EXE download was not acceptable.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Added -SkipUpload so ZIP/SHA packaging can be verified without a GitHub token.

In similar future work, apply this decision: To upload the GitHub Release assets, run npm run release:win -- -Tag v0.1.3 -ReplaceAsset after providing GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN or installing/authenticating gh with release upload capability.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Review DinoBrain data repo dry-run changes before syncing; git_sync reported 11 changed files requiring review and did not commit or push data.
Use Codex user-level UserPromptSubmit hook for global DinoBrain preflight.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep project hook as fallback and verification fixture.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Completed the global hook verification after parser hardening; this record confirmed the prompt and cwd are parsed separately from another workspace.
Use Codex user-level UserPromptSubmit hook for global DinoBrain preflight.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Keep project hook as fallback and verification fixture.
In similar future work, apply this decision: The hook command should use the PowerShell wrapper as the primary command on this Windows setup.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Reload or trust Codex project hooks before expecting live Desktop prompts to auto-trigger.
In similar future work, apply this decision: Closed the direct hook command-string verification task after confirming it emitted DinoBrain events.
